description_long: This object is a flintlock-style handgun with a short, wide barrel that flares outward at the muzzle, reminiscent of a blunderbuss pistol. The frame and barrel are finished in a dark, aged brass or bronze metal, giving it an antique appearance. The grip is made of dark wood, smoothly contoured and curving downward in a classic pistol shape, with no visible checkering or embellishments. The trigger guard is large and rounded, made of metal, and the hammer is prominently arched above the grip. There are no visible modern features such as a slide, magazine release, or safety switch, emphasizing its historical design. The overall construction appears robust, with a focus on durability and period-accurate craftsmanship.
description: Antique flintlock-style handgun with a flared brass barrel and smooth, curved wooden grip. Features a large rounded trigger guard and prominent hammer, evoking a historical design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full profile of the flared barrel, curved wooden grip, and large trigger guard.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the circular muzzle opening and the narrow width of the grip and barrel.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same flared barrel and grip, with the hammer and trigger guard visible.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the wide, flared muzzle and the alignment of the barrel with the grip behind it.
attribute_grip_design: The grip is smoothly contoured and made of dark wood, curving downward in a classic pistol shape. It lacks visible checkering, stippling, or finger grooves, presenting a polished, ergonomic surface with a natural wood grain finish. There are no logos, embellishments, or unique design elements, giving it a simple, traditional appearance.
attribute_trigger_guard_style: The trigger guard is large, rounded, and made of metal, with a smooth, continuous curve that provides ample space around the trigger. It is relatively thick and robust, lacking any undercuts, squared-off edges, or integrated accessories. The design is plain, with no visible texturing or reinforcement.
attribute_barrel_type: The barrel is short and wide, flaring outward at the muzzle in a blunderbuss style. It has a smooth, unthreaded exterior with no visible porting, fluting, or compensators. The finish is a dark, aged brass or bronze, with no apparent markings or decorative features.
attribute_slide_markings: There are no visible markings, engravings, or inscriptions on the barrel or frame. The surface is plain, with no manufacturer logos, model names, serial numbers, or decorative patterns.
attribute_magazine_base_shape: There is no visible magazine base or floorplate, as this handgun appears to be a single-shot, muzzle-loading design typical of flintlock or blunderbuss pistols.
